ddls:
CREATE TABLE "companies" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each company */,
  "rank" INTEGER /* Rank of the company based on revenue */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the company */,
  "address" TEXT /* Street address of the company */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the company is located */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the company is located */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the company is located */,
  "msa" TEXT /* Metropolitan Statistical Area (MSA) of the company */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Annual revenue of the company in millions */,
  "employees" INTEGER /* Number of employees in the company */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry classification of the company */,
  "fortune500" INTEGER /* Indicates if the company is in the Fortune 500 list */,
  "fortuneThousand" INTEGER /* Indicates if the company is in the Fortune Thousand list */,
  "ceo_name" TEXT /* Name of the CEO of the company */,
  "founded_year" INTEGER /* Year the company was founded */,
  "website_url" TEXT /* URL of the company's website */,
  "stock_ticker" TEXT /* Stock ticker symbol of the company */,
  "market_cap" REAL /* Market capitalization of the company in millions */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_companies_industry FOREIGN KEY ("industry") REFERENCES industries ("industry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "industries" (
  "industry_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each industry */,
  "industry_name" TEXT /* Name of the industry */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the industry */,
  "sector_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sector this industry belongs to */,
  "parent_industry_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent industry, if any */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("industry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "company_id" INTEGER /* ID of the company this location belongs to */,
  "address" TEXT /* Street address of the location */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the location is situated */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the location is situated */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the location is situated */,
  "msa" TEXT /* Metropolitan Statistical Area (MSA) of the location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  "headquarters_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if this is the company's headquarters */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_locations_company_id FOREIGN KEY ("company_id") REFERENCES companies ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "revenues" (
  "revenue_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each revenue record */,
  "company_id" INTEGER /* ID of the company this revenue belongs to */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the recorded revenue */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Revenue amount for that year in millions */,
  "revenue_growth_rate" REAL /* Year-over-year revenue growth rate */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Currency of the revenue amount */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("revenue_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_revenues_company_id FOREIGN KEY ("company_id") REFERENCES companies ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee record */,
  "company_id" INTEGER /* ID of the company this employee count belongs to */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the recorded employee count */,
  "employee_count" INTEGER /* Total number of employees for that year */,
  "employee_growth_rate" REAL /* Year-over-year employee growth rate */,
  "gender_ratio" TEXT /* Ratio of male to female employees */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_company_id FOREIGN KEY ("company_id") REFERENCES companies ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "markets" (
  "market_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each market segment */,
  "market_name" TEXT /* Name of the market segment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the market segment */,
  "global_reach" INTEGER /* Indicates if the market segment has a global reach */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("market_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "market_segments" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each market segment association */,
  "company_id" INTEGER /* ID of the company associated with this market segment */,
  "market_id" INTEGER /* ID of the market segment associated with the company */,
  "segment_share" REAL /* Share of the company in the market segment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_market_segments_company_id FOREIGN KEY ("company_id") REFERENCES companies ("company_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_market_segments_market_id FOREIGN KEY ("market_id") REFERENCES markets ("market_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric record */,
  "company_id" INTEGER /* ID of the company this metric belongs to */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the recorded metric */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric (e.g., customer satisfaction, market share) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "metric_unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the metric */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the metric data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_company_id FOREIGN KEY ("company_id") REFERENCES companies ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, administrator) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "company_id" INTEGER /* ID of the company being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the access occurred */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user during the access */,
  "session_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the session in minutes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_company_id FOREIGN KEY ("company_id") REFERENCES companies ("company_id")
)
